name: Ascent to the Castillo de Alange — circular by the Cerro de la Culebra
name_original: Subida al Castillo de Alange — circular por el Cerro de la Culebra
region_intro: Jogging around Alange offers a diverse landscape, characterized by the expansive Alange Reservoir and the prominent Culebra Hill, crowned by Alange Castle. The region provides a mix of relatively flat paths ideal for running along the reservoir's perimeter and more challenging trails with significant elevation changes leading to the castle. This varied terrain, set amidst the convergence of three rivers, ensures a dynamic experience for runners. Best jogging routes around Alange The most popular jogging route is Route Peñas Blancas by the Minas de la Zaza — circular from La Zarza, a 11.0 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete, offering a challenging route through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to the Castillo de Alange — circular by the Cerro de la Culebra, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path, offering panoramic views from the historic castle. Local runners also love the Torremejía and Vía de la Plata Loop, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail passing through the historic Vía de la Plata, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Jogging around Alange is defined by reservoir-side paths, trails ascending Culebra Hill, and routes through the surrounding countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more difficult ascents. The routes in Alange are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 2 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Alange's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Alange?
A: Alange offers a wide selection of running routes, with nearly 50 different trails to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy jogs to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Alange?
A: The terrain around Alange is quite diverse. You'll find relatively flat and scenic paths ideal for running along the perimeter of the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking a challenge, there are also trails with significant elevation changes leading up to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt; on Culebra Hill, offering rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Alange?
A: Yes, while many routes offer a moderate to difficult challenge, there is at least one easy jogging route available. The area's diverse landscape means you can find paths that suit a more relaxed pace, particularly along the reservoir.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Alange?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Alange are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282185327/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-alange-circular-by-the-cerro-de-la-culebra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Alange — circular by the Cerro de la Culebra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that forms a loop.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running in Alange?
A: Alange's routes are rich with scenic views and historical landmarks. You can enjoy breathtaking panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt; on Culebra Hill, overlooking the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; themselves are a major highlight, and you might even pass by the ancient Roman thermal baths, a UNESCO World Heritage site, adding a cultural element to your run.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails around Alange?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, Alange offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle – Views of the Alange Reservoir loop from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route stretching over 45 kilometers, providing an extensive experience through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Alange?
A: The running routes in Alange are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars. Over 300 runners have explored the area using komoot, often praising the diverse terrain and the stunning views, particularly around the reservoir and from the castle.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through historical areas?
A: Yes, Alange is steeped in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282179782/torremejia-and-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torremejía and Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you along sections of the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road. Additionally, routes ascending to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt; offer a direct connection to the region's medieval past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Alange?
A: While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, Alange's climate, typical of the Tierra de Barros region, generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities during spring and autumn. Summers can be quite warm, making early mornings or late evenings preferable for running, while winters are usually mild.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of vineyards or agricultural landscapes?
A: Yes, the region around Alange is known for its agricultural heritage. You might encounter views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4082806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Ribera del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, and there's even a specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/742763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyard Road on the Camino de Almendralejo to Torremejía&lt;/a&gt; that offers a glimpse into the local viticulture while you run.

Q: How challenging are the running routes in Alange?
A: The routes in Alange offer a good mix of challenges. While there's one easy route, the majority are classified as moderate (28 routes) or difficult (17 routes). This means you can find everything from gentle reservoir-side jogs to demanding ascents with significant elevation gain, such as those leading to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: At the top of the Culebra hill is the Alange castle, a strategic fortress from the 9th century and the site of battles for Arabs and Christians.

The Alange Reservoir is an impressive engineering work located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, near the town of Alange and about 20 km from Mérida.
The concrete dam was inaugurated in 1992 and is located on the Matachel River, close to its mouth in the Guadiana River.
